sections.subtitle: Match Overview
sections.text: Venezia, currently languishing at 19th in the league with 26 points, face a tough challenge against Fiorentina, who sit comfortably at 9th with 59 points. This matchup at the Stadio Pierluigi Penzo in Venice is set to be a clash of contrasting fortunes. Venezia will be desperate to climb out of the relegation zone, while Fiorentina aim to solidify their mid-table position. The referee, Matteo Marchetti, averages 4.2 yellow cards per game, so expect a potentially feisty encounter. Additionally, Marchetti has a red card average of 0.27 per game, indicating a slight chance of a dismissal.

sections.subtitle: Historical Context
sections.text: Looking at their last 5 encounters, Fiorentina have dominated with 3 wins, while Venezia managed just 1 victory, with 1 draw between them. Their most recent clash on August 25, 2024, ended in a 0-0 stalemate, with Venezia’s goalkeeper Jesse Joronen earning a stellar 9.2 Sofascore rating as the Player of the Match. Head-to-head trends show a tendency for low-scoring games, with under 2.5 goals in all 5 of their last meetings. Moreover, discipline has been maintained in these fixtures, as under 4.5 cards were recorded in 8 of their last 10 encounters.

sections.subtitle: Team Form and Streaks
sections.text: Both teams are currently on a 3-game winless streak, adding extra pressure to this fixture. Venezia have failed to keep a clean sheet in their last 3 home games, while Fiorentina have not managed a shutout in their last 6 away matches. Fiorentina’s away games often see over 2.5 goals in 5 of their last 6 outings, with both teams scoring in those matches as well. On the other hand, Venezia’s home games tend to finish under 2.5 goals in 5 of their last 6. Additionally, Fiorentina have been first to concede in 4 of their last 5 away fixtures, while their games often see over 4.5 cards in 4 of 5 matches.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch - Fiorentina
sections.text: Fiorentina boast some standout performers, notably Moise Kean, who ranks 2nd in the league for goals with 17 strikes from an expected goals value of 16.44. Another key player is Yacine Adli, with 4 goals and 5 assists, and an impressive average Sofascore rating of 7.07. Robin Gosens, currently in form with a 7.52 average rating over the last 5 matches, has contributed 3 goals and 1 assist, making him a significant threat from defense. Albert Guðmundsson, with 6 goals, also adds depth to their attack. However, they’ll miss players like Danilo Cataldi and Nicolò Zaniolo, which could impact their midfield creativity.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch - Venezia
sections.text: For Venezia, Hans Nicolussi Caviglia stands out in midfield with 4 goals and 2 assists, overperforming his expected goals of 2.21. Alessio Zerbin, a standout in past matchups against Fiorentina, has an average Sofascore rating of 7.47, scoring 2 goals in his 3 starts against them. Christian Gytkjær, with 2 goals and 1 assist, will be crucial upfront despite underperforming his expected goals of 2.20. Gianluca Busio also warrants a mention in midfield with 2 goals, though he has yet to register an assist. Unfortunately, they’ll be without defender Michael Svoboda, which could weaken their backline.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights and Stats
sections.text: Venezia struggle offensively, ranking 18th with just 28 goals scored this season, and their conversion rate of big chances is a dismal 26.2%, the worst in the league at 19th. Defensively, they’ve conceded 49 goals (12th worst) but excel in tackles (15.97 per game, 6th) and clearances (23.34 per game, 5th). Fiorentina, on the other hand, score heavily in the middle periods of games, with 12 goals between the 31st and 45th minutes. Venezia’s goals often come late, with 28.57% of their strikes between the 76th and 90th minutes. Possession-wise, Venezia average 44.83% (16th), while their accurate passes percentage of 78.34% ranks 18th.

sections.subtitle: Set-Piece and Disciplinary Trends
sections.text: Corners are unlikely to be abundant, as Venezia average just 3.66 per game (19th), and both teams have seen under 10.5 corners in most recent matches (5 of 6 for Venezia, 6 of 8 for Fiorentina). Discipline could be a factor, as Fiorentina’s away games often see over 4.5 cards (4 of 5), while Venezia have accumulated 70 yellows this season (12th fewest). Venezia’s foul count stands at 463 (9th most), so expect some physicality. Fiorentina’s tendency to concede first on the road might give Venezia a chance to exploit early set-pieces or counterattacks.

sections.subtitle: Venue and Atmosphere
sections.text: The match at Stadio Pierluigi Penzo in Venice offers a unique backdrop, with its intimate setting potentially favoring the home side. Venezia will rely on their fans to create a hostile environment for Fiorentina, who have struggled defensively away from home. With only 6 clean sheets all season (16th), Venezia need to capitalize on home advantage to shore up their defense. Fiorentina, however, will look to dominate possession and exploit Venezia’s low ranking in ground duels won (47.14%, 19th) and aerial duels won (44.28%, 20th).
